etymology.description: 亻(亻) (rén, "person") as semantic + 吏(吏) (lì, "official") as phonetic -> causing or enabling something as an official or representative action
etymology.interpretation: The character 使(使) (shǐ, "cause; send; enable") combines the semantic component 亻(亻) (rén, "person"), implying an action performed by a person, with the phonetic component 吏(吏) (lì, "official"), which represents a hand holding a representative object. This combination suggests the meaning of sending or enabling as carried out by an ambassador or representative.

examples.chinese: 这种药使他的病情好转(這種藥使他的病情好轉)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng yào shǐ tā de bìngqíng hǎozhuǎn.
examples.english: This medicine caused his condition to improve.

examples.chinese: 父亲使我去城里买东西(父親使我去城里買東西)。
examples.pinyin: Fùqīn shǐ wǒ qù chéng lǐ mǎi dōngxi.
examples.english: Father sent me to the city to buy things.

examples.chinese: 新的法律使公司必须遵循更严格的标准(新的法律使公司必須遵循更嚴格的標準)。
examples.pinyin: Xīn de fǎlǜ shǐ gōngsī bìxū zūnxún gèng yángé de biāozhǔn.
examples.english: The new law requires companies to adhere to stricter standards.
